content.full_text:
State of California                                                                     Franchise Tax Board

Memorandum

To     :   Michael Bennett, Executive Director               Date  :   November 15, 1977
          Fair Political Practices Commission
          1100 K Street
          Sacramento, CA 95814                              File No.:


From   :   Robert C. Summers

Subject:   Confirmation Letter


          Enclosed is a copy of our latest confirmation letter in which we have
          incorporated some of your suggested wording.  We invite any comments
          you may have.


          [signature]
          Chief, Political Reform Audit Division

          Enc.

          cc:  Robert M. Stern

POLITICAL REFORM AUDIT DIVISION
9660 Flair Drive
El Monte, CA 91731
Telephone (213) 575-6670
//DATE          November


The Political Reform Act of 1974 provides for public disclosure of
all receipts and expenditures by state candidates and committees.
To help accomplish this objective, the Act requires that all state
candidates and committees which meet certain monetary or vote
criteria be audited.

An important but routine part of each audit is this confirmation
form which is sent to a random sample of campaign contributors to
verify monetary and nonmonetary contributions. Completion of this
form is voluntary; however, in unusual circumstances and with additional notice, the department may require that the information be
submitted. Authorization for this request for information is
derived from the requirement in Chapter 10 of the Political Reform
Act of 1974 that the Franchise Tax Board perform audits of reports
and statements filed under Chapter 4. The department maintains
audit files on candidates and/or committees, but does not maintain
them on individual contributors.

If you have any questions or comments, please direct them to the
Assistant to the District Director at the address shown above.

Your name is shown as a contributor on campaign statements filed
by _________________________________ for the
period _________________ through _________________. Please list,
in the space provided below, any monetary or nonmonetary contribution
made to the above candidate or committee during this period.


     Date               Amount                Description


     ______________    _______________     _________________________

     ______________    _______________     _________________________


                            Signature                 Date

Your prompt response will be appreciated. An envelope is enclosed
for your reply.

sections.question_synthetic: This document does not contain a legal question; it is internal correspondence regarding campaign finance disclosure and audit procedures.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is internal correspondence/transmittal letter, not an FPPC advice letter response. Contains fragmented text in enclosed materials with unclear OCR quality. No formal Q&A structure present. Document ID format (77ADV-77-539) suggests advice letter numbering but content is administrative correspondence between agencies.

embedding.summary: A November 1977 memorandum from Robert C. Summers (Franchise Tax Board Chief of Political Reform Audit Division) to Michael Bennett (FPPC Executive Director) transmitting an updated confirmation letter incorporating FPPC suggested wording, with an attached explanation of audit procedures under California's Political Reform Act.
